title: dianthus lanceolatus
intro: Dianthus lanceolatus, also known as the lance-leaved pink, is a species of flowering plant in the carnation family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been known to help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is also believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, making it a popular choice for teas and infusions. Herbal preparations of Dianthus lanceolatus often include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a variety of health concerns, from skin conditions to respiratory issues.
